explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: The 2024 proxy statement indicates board-level awareness of AI and mentions a director with technical expertise in AI. This document references board oversight of the company's technology strategy, including AI, and a commitment to informed decision-making regarding AI, as evidenced by the board's engagement with the technology.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The 2024 proxy statement indicates board-level awareness of AI through the mention of a director with technical expertise in digital transformations, cybersecurity, and AI. This supports the **governance** pillar by suggesting oversight of the company's technology strategy, including AI. The proxy statement also implies a commitment to informed decision-making regarding AI, as evidenced by the board's engagement with the technology.
relevant_sources.title: DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2024

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Expeditors International of Washington's 2024 proxy statement indicates board-level awareness of AI, an aspect of governance. This pillar is the only one of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars for which documented evidence was found, with the proxy statement also mentioning a director possessing technical expertise in digital transformations, cybersecurity, and AI. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars, based on a review of 7 publicly available sources.
